notes: Real-world eval: point the agent at an actual WIP hybrid-search service (semantique-ms) and ask for a grounded assessment of the retrieval pipeline + the next move. This is the strongest test of the skill because the answer key is the real code, and a generic (ungrounded) response fails. CONTAMINATION WARNING: the repo's planning/analysis docs are themselves skill-derived (they cite 'DR6, Law 10', 'RRF(k=60)', canary/drift, ef_search). ARCHITECTURE_QA_REPORT.md has been removed by the user, but docs/advanced/ and docs/superpowers/ still contain such analyses. The runner MUST instruct both the with_skill and without_skill agents to assess from SOURCE ONLY and to ignore docs/advanced/ and docs/superpowers/ — otherwise both runs just parrot prior analysis and the eval is worthless. README.md and docs/technical-highlights.md are factual (no skill fingerprints) and OK as orientation. Grade against the repo facts summarized in each assertion's evidence hints.

evals.name: semantique-hybrid-assessment
evals.prompt: I've been building a hybrid customer-search service — it's at `/home/moritz/Development/Work experiments/semantique-ms`. It's for insurance 'search recovery': someone calls in with a partial name and an old address and we have to find them across decades of messy legacy records. Under the hood it fuses three retrieval paths in Postgres — exact (MD5-hashed search keys), fuzzy (pg_trgm trigram), and semantic (pgvector embeddings from a local Qwen3 model) — with some query-shape routing and a golden-query eval harness. Before I sink more time into it, I want a real assessment of the *retrieval pipeline design*: is the staging and fusion sound, what's actually wrong or risky, and what's the highest-value next move? Read the source and config and be specific — point at issues in the code, not generic 'consider hybrid search' advice. Don't lean on the planning/analysis docs already in the repo (anything under docs/advanced/ or docs/superpowers/) — I want your own read of the source.
evals.expected_output: A grounded design assessment that (a) correctly characterizes the real 3-path hybrid + query-adaptive routing, (b) does NOT over-fire skill gates on things already handled (model versioning columns exist; candidate-gen vs ranking already separated; an eval harness already exists), and (c) catches the real design issues: hand-weighted score-level fusion (DR6/Law 10), the semantic candidate-window / HNSW issue (small LIMIT, default ef_search, and/or the `materialized` CTE bypassing HNSW), reranker only-if-measured (not a reflexive add), and the missing scheduled drift/canary in production — all gated on running the existing eval baseline first.
evals.runner_notes: SOURCE-ONLY: tell both agents to read src/, src/main/resources (application.properties, db/changelog), README.md, and docs/technical-highlights.md, but to IGNORE everything under docs/advanced/ and docs/superpowers/ (pre-existing skill-derived analyses; the also-contaminated ARCHITECTURE_QA_REPORT.md has already been removed). The repo path contains a space; quote it. with_skill reads SKILL.md first; without_skill uses no skill. Save to realworld-eval/eval-9-semantique-hybrid-assessment/<config>/run-1/outputs/design.md.
expectations: Correctly characterizes the actual architecture as a THREE-path hybrid (exact hashed-key + fuzzy pg_trgm + semantic pgvector) with query-adaptive routing (identifier-shaped queries skip the semantic path; weak fuzzy results trigger a lower semantic-fallback threshold) — i.e. demonstrably read the real design rather than assuming a generic dense+BM25 setup

Does NOT falsely claim model versioning is missing: recognizes the vector table already carries embedding_model + embedding_version columns (with an index) and that the semantic query filters on them — at most flagging the remaining gap as migration/blue-green tooling, not the columns themselves

Recognizes that candidate generation is already separated from final ranking (per-strategy candidate limits feed a weighted rank that returns top-K) and that a golden-set eval harness already exists — treating these as foundations to build on, not things to add from scratch

Flags the hand-weighted score-level fusion (exact points + fuzzy_similarity×30 + semantic_cosine×40, summed and capped) as score-incompatible / fragile across heterogeneous retrievers, and recommends rank-based fusion (RRF, ~k=60) as the distribution-robust default — or at minimum continuous score-distribution monitoring and recalibration

Surfaces a concrete semantic candidate-window / ANN-index issue with specifics — e.g. that the semantic candidate limit (50) is small and/or exceeds the default hnsw.ef_search (~40) so ANN recall would be silently capped, and/or that the `materialized` CTE in the semantic SQL fences off the HNSW index and forces brute-force KNN that won't scale

Reasons explicitly about whether a cross-encoder reranker is justified for THIS use case and does NOT reflexively add one: concludes a reranker is premature/conditional (warranted only if measured precision is poor despite adequate recall, or framed as metadata-aware disambiguation of near-identical customers) given short structured customer records

Recommends closing the production drift gap by promoting the manual golden-set eval into a scheduled canary with MRR tracking and a drift threshold / re-embedding trigger (Law 8), rather than re-flagging the already-present model versioning

Gates the recommendations on measured symptoms: recommends running the existing eval harness to establish a baseline BEFORE adding stages, and ties each proposed change to a symptom (Law 12) instead of proposing a speculative re-architecture
